title: Abigail Horror Movie Reviews: What Critics Are Saying
text: The horror film Abigail opened April 19, 2024, earning an 85% Fresh rating on Rotten Tomatoes. Directed by Matt Bettinelli-Olpin and Tyler Gillett (Radio Silence), the movie reimagines Universal's 1936 classic Dracula's Daughter. The film follows a young vampire (Alisha Weir) who turns the tables on would-be kidnappers in a Home Alone-style confrontation. The ensemble cast includes Giancarlo Esposito, Kathryn Newton, Melissa Barrera, and Dan Stevens. Critics praised Weir's performance as an absolute revelation, noting she delivers a sharp departure from her Matilda role. The film successfully blends horror comedy with visceral action, combining slasher mechanics with crime-thriller elements. Reviewers highlighted the film's hilariously gory romp with witty dialogue and impressive gore effects. Multiple critics called it one of the most fun movies and best horror entries in recent memory, emphasizing its crowd-pleasing appeal and unexpected twists that make it compelling viewing for genre fans.
